title: Morphology and Word Formation
description: Word structure, inflection, derivation, compounding, productivity, and lexical pattern reasoning.
upgradePremise: Expanded for Nyx and Marion with stronger grammatical reasoning, case-based analysis, modules, projects, and explicit source coverage.

forNyx: Prefer precise, grammatical phrasing that sounds natural rather than stiff.
Use register-sensitive wording so conversational output and formal output do not collapse into one style.
When uncertain, preserve meaning first, then improve form through revision rather than over-correcting.
forMarion: Use the pack to explain why a wording choice works, not just whether it is 'right' or 'wrong'.
Ground revision advice in form-function reasoning, register, audience, and evidence.
Support coaching, diagnostics, and portfolio-style improvement tracking.

topics.name: Morphemes and Productivity
coreClaims: Morphemes are meaning-bearing units (free vs bound).
Productive processes generate new forms (compounding, affixation, conversion).
practice: Segment words into morphemes and justify boundaries.
Explain why a coinage feels 'natural' or 'awkward' (productivity constraints).

topics.name: Inflection vs Derivation
coreClaims: Inflection encodes grammatical features (tense, number) without changing lexical category.
Derivation often changes meaning and/or category (e.g., verb  noun).
practice: Classify affixes and predict category output.

modules.name: Morpheme Segmentation
outcomes: Segment words plausibly and justify boundaries.
Distinguish folk etymology from current morphological analysis.

modules.name: Inflection vs Derivation
outcomes: Classify affixes and predict category output.
Explain how derivation shifts meaning or discourse role.

modules.name: Productivity and Neologisms
outcomes: Judge why a new formation feels natural, awkward, humorous, or domain-bound.

caseStudies.title: Nyx Invents Terms That Feel Off
caseStudies.scenario: The assistant creates understandable but unnatural forms because it applies productive patterns without regard to frequency, register, or lexicalization.
intervention: Check whether a coinage already exists.
Test whether the derivational pattern is productive in that semantic field.
Prefer transparent paraphrase when the coined form feels forced.

projects.name: Word Family Atlas
projects.goal: Map roots, derivations, category shifts, and register notes for high-value vocabulary families used by Nyx and Marion.

referenceTextsUsed.title: A Comprehensive Grammar of the English Language
referenceTextsUsed.citationAPA: Quirk, R., Greenbaum, S., Leech, G., & Svartvik, J. (1985). A comprehensive grammar of the English language. Longman.

referenceTextsUsed.title: Practical English Usage
referenceTextsUsed.citationAPA: Swan, M. (2016). Practical English usage (4th ed.). Oxford University Press.

issuesAddressed: uploaded_file_contained_phonetics_content_under_morphology_filename; reconstructed morphology pack from prior valid morphology source and hardened metadata
normalizationStatus.normalizedFor: Marion/Nyx domain registry and Compose ingestion
